w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: This document outlines the full product strategy, phased execution plan, and launch timeline for the
. The EPK Module is the first major initiative in a broader platform transformation that positions Songdew as the professional identity layer for India's independent music ecosystem —
"Songdew for Artists, what LinkedIn is for Professionals."

Deliver a production-ready EPK module within 5.5 weeks that gives independent artists a single, shareable professional profile — and gives industry stakeholders (curators, labels, brands) a purpose-built discovery experience.
Songdew currently presents a single dashboard to two fundamentally different user types — working artists and industry professionals. This creates friction for both.
Artists and listeners/curators share the same dashboard navigation — wrong context for both
The e-Press Kit is a sub-menu item buried inside an artist workstation; industry stakeholders cannot find it without guidance
One URL (songdew.com/profile) tries to serve the working artist AND the curious curator — neither gets an optimal experience
Manages 5+ platforms with no unified professional presence
Spends hours vetting artists across low-fidelity links
No easy way to assess cultural fit or collaboration potential
Discovers good music but cannot follow artist journeys
"Make Songdew for Artists what LinkedIn is for Professionals"
The EPK Module is the first step toward splitting Songdew into two purpose-built surfaces:
EPK builder, releases, analytics, promo tools
Read-only EPK view, artist search, booking CTA
Portfolio showcase + skill discovery + client pitching
Four core user profiles inform the EPK feature set and UX decisions. Developed via 2–3 user interviews and pattern-matching against existing Songdew artist data.
One link for music, press, booking, story
"Send one link and be taken seriously"
Hours vetting artists across low-fidelity links
Evaluate stage history, press, music in < 60 seconds
"Show me stage experience and I'll book"
No way to assess cultural fit or collab potential
Social reach, press, professional enquiry type
Discovers good music but can't follow artist journeys
Discovery feed, follow artists, new releases
"Follow them like I follow on Instagram"
14 features scoped and prioritised using MoSCoW methodology. P0 features are launch blockers; P1 features ship with v1.0.
Research conducted over 2 days using AI-assisted synthesis (Perplexity, Gemini) and direct platform analysis.
No media, no credibility, no booking flow
No EPK, no press, no industry targeting
Fills all identified gaps — our opportunity
The EPK Module is delivered in 6 sequential phases over approximately 5.5 weeks.
Feature Registry + MoSCoW Table + Scope Sign-off
14 features, full test coverage, Vitest CI
Artist Hero + banner layout, tab navigation, single-page scroll wireframe
Outfit/Poppins typography, #007BFF palette, neumorphic card system, Lucide icons
Hero header, section cards (Music, Gallery), edit modals, Profile Strength sidebar
Public EPK scroll layout, contact grid, Business Enquiries, mobile breakpoints
Figma component library, CSS variables export, redlines, stakeholder prototype
ArtistContext + state management, Navbar Edit/View toggle, Hero Header (Banner + Avatar), Social Links row + modals, Tabs navigation
Story Section + bio editor, Music Section (tracks + releases), Gallery (videos + photos), Section Visibility toggle, Manage Sections modal
Achievements, Press, Assets, Business Enquiries section, Sidebar + contact edit modal, Profile Strength gamification, Back-to-top + toast system
Vitest + @testing-library/react | Unit tests for Context | Component tests for Edit Mode
Day-by-day schedule across 5.5 weeks. Each row shows the task, phase category, duration, and start/end days.
AI-assisted tooling and modern frameworks substantially compress traditional estimates:
Perplexity/Gemini synthesise 10 competitors in hours, not days
2–3 user calls + pattern-match against existing Songdew artist data
Low-fi sketches + Figma rapid wireframing with pre-defined design system
Next.js + Tailwind + React Context = pre-solved infrastructure; focus on logic
Vitest + Testing Library makes TDD fast; tests written alongside features
Lighthouse automated audits + stakeholder review on pre-built prototype
Three objectives with measurable Key Results, directly tied to discovery, stakeholder friction, and booking conversion.
% of artists reaching 'Discovery Ready' status within 48 hours of EPK sign-up
60%+ artists reach 'Discovery Ready' (≥80% profile strength) within 48h of signup
35% of active users reach 100% Profile Strength within 90 days
55% of artists add ≥1 release in first session
Curator survey: 50% reduction in vetting time vs. pre-EPK baseline
Bounce rate from profile pages (curator segment) < 40%
EPK Asset downloads ≥ 8 per 1,000 profile views
Booking enquiries via EPK: +30% month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
Blob URL expiry on browser reload
v1: Show re-upload prompt. v2: Migrate to S3/Cloudinary CDN upload on save.
Optimise serialisation; defer large assets to API-backed persistence in v2.
. Monitor edge cases. Defer non-critical animations.
Lazy-load Assets section. next/image optimisation. File size preview before download trigger.
Framer Motion layout system handles alignment. 1 dedicated QA day built into each sprint.
Prototype shared at Day 10 for early feedback. Iterate before dev sprint begins.
Server components, file-based routing, image optimisation built-in
Zero-dependency, sufficient scope for EPK data; avoids Redux overhead
Utility-first, design-token driven, no runtime overhead
Layout animations and edit-mode transitions with minimal code
icon set aligned to Songdew brand
Fast unit/component tests; co-located with features
Automated performance, accessibility, and SEO audits on each PR
Zero-config Next.js deployment with preview URLs per branch
Component library, wireframes, dev handoff, stakeholder review
Upon approval of this document, the following actions will be initiated immediately:
Approval — This doc + deck shared with team lead for sign-off. Feedback incorporated within 1 day.
— Begin Market Research phase. Set up project board in Notion/Jira with sprint structure.
Weekly Sync — 30-min weekly check-in to review progress, surface blockers, adjust timeline.
Day-10 Preview — Design prototype demo + stakeholder feedback session before dev sprint.
Soft Launch — EPK v1.0 bundled with Songdew Pro. Campaign: 'Your EPK is your Passport to the Stage'.
